description_long: This is a large, modern utility helicopter with a streamlined fuselage and a prominent main rotor system featuring five long, dark blades. The body is painted in a light gray color with subtle panel lines and a few darker accents, giving it a military or law enforcement appearance. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular windows providing wide visibility for the pilots. The tail section extends back to a horizontal stabilizer and a tail rotor, which is mounted on the right side and features four short blades. The helicopter is equipped with retractable landing gear rather than traditional skids, and various hatches and access panels are visible along the fuselage. The overall design suggests a robust, multi-purpose aircraft suitable for urban operations such as transport, rescue, or surveillance.
description: A large, light gray utility helicopter with five-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and retractable landing gear. The design is robust and modern, suitable for urban transport or rescue.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, angular cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the five main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the full length of the fuselage, the main rotor, tail boom, and the landing gear beneath the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, horizontal stabilizer, and the tail rotor on the right side.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, main rotor, tail section, and the landing gear, with more detail on the access panels.
attribute_rotor_blades: Five long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, colored black with light gray tips, no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Four-blade tail rotor mounted vertically on the right side of the tail, colored black with light gray tips, no protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: No landing skids; helicopter is equipped with retractable landing gear consisting of wheels and struts, painted gray.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, angular cockpit windows at the front, slightly tinted, framed in black, providing wide visibility.
